validation_commits.summary: workflow artifact commit; no new target-facing runtime contract beyond planning evidence
tiered rule schema and init taxonomy
sectioned rule selection flow, audit logging, doctor compatibility, tool registration
docs for the cognitive alignment protocol

project_type.implementation_repo: pnpm TypeScript monorepo with CLI, MCP server, shared schemas, and Vitest-based tests
project_type.target_repo: single-package Cocos Creator 3.8 TypeScript project with gameplay code under assets/script and content/config assets under assets/resources
project_type.e2e_goal: validate the new Fabric cognitive alignment protocol against a real external app repo without risking user work

fabric_v2: manual MCP or HTTP smoke validation for final E2E
oops_framework: no automated Node test framework detected
Cocos Creator project structure suggests manual or tool-driven integration validation rather than npm test scripts

commands: pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server exec vitest run src/index.test.ts src/services/plan-context.test.ts src/services/rule-sections.test.ts src/services/audit-log.test.ts src/services/doctor.test.ts src/tools/rule-sections.test.ts

package_manager.secondary_marker: yarn.lock also present, so team history may be mixed

notes: No npm/yarn build or test scripts were detected in package.json.
Actual write validation should happen only in a disposable filesystem copy of the current working tree.

affected_fabric_files.why_it_matters: E2E must confirm .fabric/INITIAL_TAXONOMY.md is planned and written in the right non-destructive mode.

affected_fabric_files.surface: shared agents meta schema and stable_id-only description identity
affected_fabric_files.why_it_matters: The registry shape now treats stable_id as the only rule identity and drives plan-context/rule-sections contracts.

affected_fabric_files.surface: fab_plan_context neutral profile, description index, and selection token
affected_fabric_files.why_it_matters: E2E must verify neutral requirement_profile output, required vs selectable stable_ids, and selection_token issuance.

affected_fabric_files.surface: fab_get_rule_sections section fetch, hard errors, and warning diagnostics
affected_fabric_files.why_it_matters: E2E must prove server-side merge of required plus AI-selected rules, hard failures for invalid selections, and warning-only behavior for missing sections.

affected_fabric_files.surface: rule_selection audit and doctor audit compatibility
affected_fabric_files.why_it_matters: E2E must confirm rule_selection events are written to .fabric/audit.jsonl and accepted by fabric doctor --audit.
Server registration must expose fab_plan_context and fab_get_rule_sections while the new protocol remains consistent with serve/http behavior.
The live user-facing contract now instructs plan-context then section fetch, not the legacy get-rules-first path.

fabric_presence.interpretation: The target repo appears partially prepared for Fabric but not actually initialized.

working_tree.summary: The repo has a large volume of pre-existing uncommitted changes across assets, settings, package manifests, docs, and scripts.

candidate_validation_paths.reason: good bootstrap/global path for initial plan-context smoke validation
top-level runtime entry point; likely to exercise broad L0 plus app-level L1/L2 routing
closest business path to initialization and flow orchestration
UI/config domain candidate for L1 selection behavior
resource/config domain candidate with narrow local scope
non-code path to confirm docs handling and neutral description output

e2e_target_state.safe_write_strategy: Keep the current repo read-only. For any real init or reapply run, make a disposable filesystem copy of the current working tree and validate there.

candidate_e2e_flow.name: Preflight snapshot in place
actions: Record git status --short in /mnt/c/Project/oops-framework.
Confirm that .fabric exists but is incomplete and currently lacks agents.meta/bootstrap README/taxonomy.
candidate_e2e_flow.goal: Establish that the source repo is unsafe for direct write validation.

candidate_e2e_flow.name: Read-only init planning in place
actions: Use the locally built Fabric CLI and run fabric init --plan in the target repo.
Capture whether Fabric treats the repo as uninitialized or partial-reapply state.
candidate_e2e_flow.goal: Validate planning behavior without touching user work.

candidate_e2e_flow.name: Disposable copy for write validation
actions: Create a filesystem copy of /mnt/c/Project/oops-framework that preserves the current dirty working tree.
Do not use git stash, reset, or a clean worktree because that would drop or rewrite user changes.
candidate_e2e_flow.goal: Provide a realistic but safe E2E sandbox.

candidate_e2e_flow.name: Scaffold validation in disposable copy
actions: Build Fabric from /mnt/c/Project/fabric-v2 with pnpm -r build.
Run the built CLI against the disposable copy with init or init --reapply --yes depending on step-2 output.
Verify .fabric/INITIAL_TAXONOMY.md, .fabric/bootstrap/README.md, .fabric/agents.meta.json, and .fabric/forensic.json.
candidate_e2e_flow.goal: Validate the c4d957a init/taxonomy surface end-to-end.

candidate_e2e_flow.name: Project rule completion
actions: Continue the Fabric initialization handoff in an MCP-capable client so project-specific rules are populated.
Ensure the disposable copy contains enough rule nodes and rule content to exercise L1 selection and L2 local matching.
candidate_e2e_flow.goal: Avoid a shallow bootstrap-only test that would miss the cognitive alignment surfaces.

candidate_e2e_flow.name: plan-context smoke checks
actions: Start fabric serve against the disposable copy.
Call fab_plan_context for README.md, assets/script/Main.ts, assets/script/game/initialize/Initialize.ts, and assets/script/game/common/config/GameUIConfig.ts.
Assert revision_hash, stale behavior, selection_token, neutral requirement_profile, required_stable_ids, ai_selectable_stable_ids, and description_index.
candidate_e2e_flow.goal: Validate the new neutral planning contract from c7b1988.

candidate_e2e_flow.name: rule section and failure-path checks
actions: Call fab_get_rule_sections with MANDATORY_INJECTION and CONTEXT_INFO using valid L1 selections.
Confirm missing sections return empty strings plus warning diagnostics only.
Probe hard-error paths: expired or unknown selection_token, invalid L1 selection, selecting required-only ids, and missing ai_selection_reasons.
candidate_e2e_flow.goal: Validate sectioned retrieval semantics and guarded failure behavior.

candidate_e2e_flow.name: Audit and doctor compatibility checks
actions: Verify .fabric/audit.jsonl receives rule_selection events after successful section fetches.
Create a minimal staged edit or equivalent auditable change in the disposable copy.
Run fabric doctor --audit and confirm rule_selection satisfies the preceding-rule-access requirement.
candidate_e2e_flow.goal: Validate audit-log and doctor compatibility for the new protocol.

risks_and_safety_notes: Do not run fabric init --reapply --yes in /mnt/c/Project/oops-framework directly. The target repo has extensive unrelated uncommitted changes.
A git worktree is not sufficient for preserving the current dirty state because worktrees track committed history, not the present working copy.
The safest write strategy is a plain filesystem copy of the target repo before any Fabric write validation.
The current target already has empty .fabric, .agents, .claude, .codex, and .husky directories. That partial state may affect init/reapply branching and should be captured during the plan-only pass.

Because .fabric/agents.meta.json is currently missing, fab_plan_context and fab_get_rule_sections are not meaningfully testable on the target repo until scaffold plus project-rule completion are done.
Commit 916d3fe is mostly workflow evidence and should not drive E2E assertions by itself; runtime validation should focus on c4d957a, c7b1988, and 383fefc.
Tool-registration validation should explicitly confirm fab_get_rule_sections is present and the old MCP edit loop no longer depends on fab_get_rules as the primary path.
The target repo has no npm test/build scripts, so E2E success criteria must be protocol-level rather than package-script-level.

L0.goal: Static preflight and no-write safety checks
checks: Map the changed Fabric files and protocol docs to expected runtime behavior.
Inspect target repo package markers, .fabric state, and dirty working tree.
Run only read-only commands in the original target repo.

L1.goal: Fabric repo unit and integration confidence
checks: Run shared schema tests for stable_id-only description behavior.
Run CLI init tests for taxonomy generation and non-destructive behavior.
Run server tests for plan-context, rule-sections, audit-log, doctor, and tool registration.
Validate init plan and actual scaffold writes in a disposable copy of oops-framework.
Start fabric serve against the copy and exercise fab_plan_context on representative target files.
Exercise fab_get_rule_sections for both success and warning cases.

L3.goal: Full behavioral E2E with audit compatibility
checks: Use real project-specific rules in the disposable copy, not bootstrap-only metadata.
Verify rule_selection audit entries are written after section fetches.
Confirm fabric doctor --audit accepts the new rule_selection flow and remains compatible with the updated edit loop.
